title: Why doesnt Asuka or Wille go for Instrumentality
display_title: Why doesnt Asuka or Wille go for Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Why doesn't Asuka (or Wille) go for Instrumentality?             Quote Post by riplay » Tue Jun 25, 2013 5:35 pm I mean, she had a terrible childhood, lots of shit happened while piloting the Eva, will stay in a 15 yo body all her life... She doesn't look happy at all in 3.33; so why doesn't she go for Instrumentality? If I was her I would totally support SEELE...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jun 25, 2013 5:52 pm We don't even exactly know what SEELE's Instrumentality is in Rebuild, we only had vague hints from Kaworu that it included sacrificing mankind to create a new race with the Fruit of Life, not to fuse everyone in one giant consciousness like in NGE. Besides SEELE is kinda imposing their perceived fate for mankind to the Lilin, and Asuka tends to hate when people impose things on her and don't let her chose her destiny, and it goes the same for the rest of WILLE.

[]:             Quote Post by riplay » Wed Jun 26, 2013 4:35 am Quite good, so it could be among other things the anti-authoritarianism of Asuka that makes her fight SEELE ;). It would be a sad thing for me if instrumentality, in rebuild, is not the one in NGE ; that's the reason why I like SEELE... we can still interpret the "new race" thing as the merging of consciousness, it is an evolved form of life after all... Actually I thought automatically that the mini-earth Kaworu shows to Shinji is a mini-instrumentality (NGE style), which contains the souls that had the time to enter into it (The FOI being the ones that did not have the time, and there's a lot of it considering the short opening of the doors of Guf). Funny how you think something is obvious and people actually have a complete other idea... :] Also another thing, in NGE Anno was calling in Japanese the complementarity project (translated as such in the early mangas and anime in the western world). Then Anno chose to call it Instrumentality to make hommage to an author or something, from what I have read... Would be interesting to know what's the word used in Japanese for Rebuild... But I'm sure some people already checked it.... Do you know where is the thread concerning the mini-earth?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jun 26, 2013 4:43 am Actually, the "mini-Earth" is the Moon, yes our big white Moon where Kaworu was living in 1.0 and 2.0! We don't know how, but Third Impact made it crash on Earth, where it began to spin widely and sucked part of Earth's atmosphere! More about it here : http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13796/Geofront-Moon-Stuff/

[]:             Quote Post by riplay » Wed Jun 26, 2013 5:53 am Nowai :o but why is it looking so strange then... I don't really like this interpretation, earth wouldn't really survive without the moon in orbit... Also it has the same pattern of the big ball that takes the souls of people in EoE... So it's still to interpretations I think. I'll add that to the topic you gave me.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 26, 2013 5:57 am Ask such questions in that thread please. (Or trudge through it for your answers.)

[]:             Quote Post by riplay » Wed Jun 26, 2013 6:20 am Yes, sorry! ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Wed Jun 26, 2013 8:21 am As crappy as her life's been, Asuka demonstrates a very strong sense of self. She does things solo if she can and wants to hold herself up. I think Asuka's personality is just antithetical to instrumentality. Even when she was subjected to it in End of Evangelion, she was one of the first people to return.

[]:             Quote Post by riplay » Wed Jun 26, 2013 8:24 am Seems legit! Thanks

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Wed Jun 26, 2013 12:34 pm If Instrumentality is anything like it is in EoE, the idea of Asuka sharing a hive-mind with the rest of humanity well past the "heat death" of the universe sounds worse than Fred Phelps' idea of Hell.

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Wed Jun 26, 2013 1:00 pm Also, on top of what people have said, there hasn't actually even been an attempt at the Human Instrumentality Project in Rebuild yet. People seem to fail to understand that an Impact and the act of Instrumentality are not the same thing. An Impact is when a Seed of Life; Adam or Lilith; wipe out all life on the planet from the other progenitor to make way for their own lifeforms to evolve. The First Impact involved any Adam based lifeforms that may've come about already being destroyed by Lilith and Adam being held in stasis by Lilith's Spear. The Second Impact was Adam awaking and destroying all Lilin life down the to the microbial level at the North Pole, as well as indirectly killing over two billion Lilin humans before Adam was somehow degraded to a embryo form. The Third Impact in EoE is a special case, wherein a godhead that was the combination of Lilith, Adam, Rei, Kaworu, Shinji and Yui decided the fate of humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jun 27, 2013 11:00 am Because sanity?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Feb 28, 2014 8:32 pm I've talked about this before. . . why not just let instrumentality happen? If N3I really has jacked up the planet and pushed humanity to the brink of extinction (at least as far as we know). Whats worse? Giving mankind a sliver of hope of continuing to exist. . . at least in some transcendent form? Or rejecting instrumentality, only to have mankind still go extinct and it be as if we never existed at all? I'll paraphrase one of my favorite tragic villains, Nox from Wakfu in an attempt to give my point. "If it works everyone will forgive me! and if it doesn't. . . well. . . I never wanted to live in this world anyway."

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Mar 01, 2014 7:53 am ^ If WILLE are still fighting against Instrumentality, it means that they think that mankind still has a chance to survive even after Third Impact. In fact, the mere fact that WILLE and all its equipment even exists is an indication that the Lilin are indeed managing to survive and somewhat thrive. From here, why would they accept the Singularity offered by the very people responsible for all this shit to begin with?(which goes as far back as 2I 29 years ago) Especially since they didn't even asked for their agreement in this. That's why Asuka, WILLE as a whole and probably most of the surviving Lilins, are against Instrumentality, if the people outside of the high-ranking members of WILLE even know the existence of the HIP.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sat Mar 01, 2014 5:39 pm Or it could all be one last-ditch desperate effort to get revenge on the man who destroyed the world. Ever watch the Animatrix? Humanity in "2nd Renaissance" put all of its remaining resources into one last ditch effort suicide mission to defeat the machines despite the fact that they couldn't possibly win. The scenario could very well be like that in Post N3I earth with the Wunder and Wille. But until 4.0 comes out were likely just guessing from what we know. (Of course Eva was an influence on the Matrix. . . so it appears we've come full circle in that respect) I wonder if someone in Final will call Misato and Wille out on this apparent hypocrisy, or at least have it addressed in some way. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Mar 01, 2014 6:24 pm But if it's really a last-ditch effort for a Pyhrric victory, then what would be the point for Kaworu to repeat in 3.0 that there's always hop, what would be the point for us, the audience, to even care for the fate of the characters if everything is already lost? Also, I don't think Misato sank that low to go all Commissar on people who disagree, else again, why would we care for what happens to her or WILLE, if they're all as rotten as Gendo?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sat Mar 01, 2014 6:26 pm NOBODY is as bad as Gendo. That's the only reason I'm willing to give Misato and Wille the benefit of a doubt. Kaworu. . .is an enigma. Not to mention he apparently deceived Shinji. Albeit with benign intentions. Much like we have to take what Fuyutsuki and Gendo said with a grain of salt, I feel we should do the same with Kaworu's talk of hope. He talked about it before he realized he had been deceived by Gendo, and that it was not his destiny to bring Shinji happiness or bring back all who had died in N3I.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Mar 01, 2014 6:29 pm ElMariachi wrote: But if it's really a last-ditch effort for a Pyhrric victory, then what would be the point for Kaworu to repeat in 3.0 that there's always hop, what would be the point for us, the audience, to even care for the fate of the characters if everything is already lost?

[]: Re: Why doesn't Asuka go for Instrumentality?             Quote Post by airman4 » Sat Mar 01, 2014 6:57 pm riplay wrote: I mean, she had a terrible childhood, lots of shit happened while piloting the Eva, will stay in a 15 yo body all her life... She doesn't look happy at all in 3.33; so why doesn't she go for Instrumentality? If I was her I would totally support SEELE...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Mar 01, 2014 7:09 pm Ray's 'speaker' was a wise man. Misato would have to shoot me next.
